facts.value: AWS Project Rainier (New Carlisle, IN) — ~2,200 MW (2.2 GW) total power draw
facts.excerpt: one site in Indiana will span thirty datacenters at 200,000 square feet apiece. This facility alone was recently reported to consume upwards of 2.2 gigawatts of power.
facts.notes: Quantifies the power of the AWS-owned Project Rainier campus that Anthropic rents → cloud_partnership, NOT Anthropic-owned power_capacity_mw. Conservative low end 2.2 GW = 2,200 MW; some trackers (aidatacenterindex.com) cite 2.4 GW — used the lower figure. 30 buildings, ~6M sq ft, ~$11B AWS investment.

verification.verifier_note: from gap-review pass; pending blind verification

facts.excerpt: world's largest cluster of non-Nvidia AI chips, with just under a million Trainium2 in the largest campus... over 1.3GW of IT capacity across three AWS campuses.
facts.notes: Third-party SemiAnalysis estimate: 'just under a million' Trainium2 accelerators in the largest AWS campus; >1.3 GW IT capacity across three AWS campuses in final construction. Value kept as text since only a 'just under a million' range is given (conservative, not rounded up).

verification.verifier_note: Semianalysis (Sep 3, 2025) states 'just under a million Trainium2 in the largest campus' (number matches), BUT it describes the campuses as in 'final construction stages', not operational. Status mismatch: under-construction, not operational as of Sep 2025. Page also does not literally name New Carl

facts.excerpt: up to one million TPUs... well over a gigawatt of capacity online in 2026... investment worth tens of billions of dollars.
facts.notes: Google Cloud-owned TPU capacity rented by Anthropic; recorded as cloud_partnership not power_capacity_mw. Capacity of 'well over a gigawatt' comes online in 2026 (>1000 MW).

verification.verifier_note: Page (Oct 23, 2025) states 'expand our use of Google Cloud technologies, including up to one million TPUs'. Number and date confirmed. Phrased as 'plan to expand' (a committed expansion), consistent with contracted.

facts.value: AWS Project Rainier (New Carlisle / St. Joseph County, Indiana)
facts.excerpt: Project Rainier is now fully operational... Nearly half a million Trainium2 chips... Anthropic is actively using Project Rainier to build and deploy... Claude.
facts.notes: AWS-OWNED facility rented by Anthropic (its primary training partner) — NOT Anthropic-owned capacity, hence recorded as cloud_partnership. Amazon $11B campus, 1,200 acres, 30 buildings of 200,000 sq ft; The Register (2025-07-04) reports the campus will draw 'upwards of 2.2 gigawatts of power'. Plan to scale to over 1 million Trainium2 chips by end of year.

verification.verifier_note: Page (aboutamazon Project Rainier) states Project Rainier is 'now fully operational' and 'Anthropic is actively using Project Rainier to build and deploy Claude'. It names 'St. Joseph County, Indiana—one of the Project Rainier sites'. New Carlisle is not literally named, but it lies in St. Joseph Co

facts.excerpt: $50 billion investment in American computing infrastructure... in partnership with Fluidstack... approximately 800 permanent jobs and 2,400 construction jobs... sites coming online throughout 2026.
facts.notes: Anthropic's own announced capex figure ($50B) for US infrastructure with Fluidstack, starting Texas + New York. Described as Anthropic's first major data-center build-out taken on directly rather than solely through cloud partners.

verification.verifier_note: Same page states 'a $50 billion investment in American computing infrastructure, building data centers with Fluidstack'. $50,000,000,000 value, announced status, and Nov 12, 2025 date all confirmed.

facts.value: New York (Fluidstack site) — part of $50B build
facts.excerpt: $50 billion investment in American computing infrastructure... data centers with Fluidstack in Texas and New York... come online throughout 2026.
facts.notes: Fluidstack-built/operated custom site; Anthropic is funder/tenant not owner, so no power_capacity_mw recorded. Secondary reporting (DCD/others) identifies the NY site as a ~360 MW Fluidstack-TeraWulf project at Lake Mariner — that 360 MW figure is NOT confirmed from a fetched primary/allowed source and should be verified before use.

verification.verifier_note: Anthropic $50B page (Nov 12, 2025) states 'building data centers with Fluidstack in Texas and New York, with more sites to come'. New York Fluidstack site as part of the build is confirmed; announced status and date match.

facts.value: Microsoft Azure — $30 billion Azure compute commitment
facts.excerpt: Anthropic has committed to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity
facts.notes: Rented capacity from Microsoft (not Anthropic-owned) → recorded as cloud_partnership. Deal also includes Anthropic contracting additional compute capacity up to one gigawatt. Announced at Microsoft Ignite, Nov 18 2025.

verification.verifier_note: from gap-review pass; pending blind verification

facts.value: NVIDIA — up to 1,000 MW (1 GW) Grace Blackwell & Vera Rubin compute
facts.excerpt: up to one gigawatt of compute capacity with NVIDIA Grace Blackwell and Vera Rubin systems
facts.notes: First direct NVIDIA compute commitment (existing dataset only had AWS/Google/Broadcom silicon). Rented capacity → cloud_partnership, NOT owned power. 1 GW = 1,000 MW (upper bound; source says 'up to'). Same announcement: NVIDIA to invest up to $10B and Microsoft up to $5B into Anthropic (equity, not infra capex).

verification.verifier_note: from gap-review pass; pending blind verification

facts.excerpt: over 1 million H100e by end of 2025... 1.4 GW in capacity at the end of 2025, around 70% of OpenAI's 1.9 GW total.
facts.notes: Third-party Epoch AI estimate of Anthropic's total accelerator fleet in H100-equivalents (Trainium + Nvidia + TPU, rented from AWS and Google). 'Over 1 million' is a floor; 1.4 GW total capacity also cited by Epoch (per internal OpenAI memo).

verification.verifier_note: Epoch (May 20, 2026) states 'this points to 1 million or more H100e for Anthropic by the end of 2025' for the total fleet. Operational-by-end-2025 estimate confirmed; note it is phrased as an estimate ('1 million or more H100e').

facts.excerpt: around 500,000 H100-equivalents (H100e) in Trainium2 chips at the end of 2025... housed in Amazon's Project Rainier campus in Indiana.
facts.notes: Third-party estimate by Epoch AI. Units are H100-equivalents (H100e), not literal GPUs — measures Trainium2 fleet in the AWS Project Rainier campus at end of 2025.

verification.verifier_note: Epoch (May 20, 2026) states 'around 500,000 H100-equivalents (H100e) in Trainium2 chips at the end of 2025', backward-looking = operational. Nuance: the figure is H100-equivalents of Trainium2, not literal GPUs, and the page does not literally name New Carlisle (though Trainium2/Rainier is deployed

facts.value: Google + Broadcom — multiple gigawatts next-gen TPU
facts.excerpt: multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity... we expect to come online starting in 2027.
facts.notes: Google/Broadcom-provided TPU capacity (rented) — cloud_partnership. No specific GW figure disclosed ('multiple gigawatts'). date_published per page as read; publication month should be re-verified.

verification.verifier_note: Page (Apr 6, 2026) states 'signed a new agreement with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity'. Value, announced status, and date confirmed.

facts.excerpt: multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity... partnership with Google and Broadcom.
facts.notes: RECLASSIFIED from custom_silicon per owner-attribution: this is a PARTNER-designed chip Anthropic uses, not Anthropic-designed silicon. Anthropic partnership with Broadcom (and Google) for next-generation custom TPU silicon. Chip designed by Google/Broadcom, not Anthropic.

verification.verifier_note: Page (Apr 6, 2026) states 'agreement with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity'. TPUs (co-designed with Broadcom) are custom silicon, so custom_silicon=true is substantively supported, though the page does not use the literal phrase 'custom silicon'.

facts.value: Amazon AWS — up to 5 GW Trainium capacity
facts.excerpt: up to 5 gigawatts (GW) of capacity for training and deploying Claude... nearly 1GW total of Trainium2 and Trainium3 capacity coming online by the end of 2026... committing more than $100 billion over
facts.notes: AWS-owned/operated capacity leased by Anthropic; recorded as cloud_partnership not power_capacity_mw. Anthropic states it currently uses 'over one million Trainium2 chips' (Anthropic's own claim, not a third-party fleet estimate). $100B AWS spend commitment over 10 years. Conservative low end of stated range: up to 5 GW; nearly 1 GW by end 2026.

verification.verifier_note: Anthropic-Amazon page (Apr 20, 2026) states 'secure up to 5 gigawatts (GW) of capacity for training and deploying Claude' spanning 'Graviton and Trainium2 through Trainium4 chips'. Signed 10-year commitment = contracted. Value, status, and date all confirmed.

facts.excerpt: nearly 1GW total of Trainium2 and Trainium3 capacity coming online by the end of 2026... currently use over one million Trainium2 chips to train and serve Claude.
facts.notes: RECLASSIFIED from custom_silicon per owner-attribution: this is a PARTNER-designed chip Anthropic uses, not Anthropic-designed silicon. Anthropic runs Claude on AWS custom silicon (Trainium2/Trainium3, with Trainium4 in the multi-generation commitment) and co-optimizes for it. Not an Anthropic-designed chip; AWS/Annapurna is the designer.

verification.verifier_note: Anthropic-Amazon page (Apr 20, 2026) explicitly references custom silicon: 'Graviton and Trainium2 through Trainium4 chips, with the option to purchase future generations of Amazon's custom silicon'. custom_silicon=true, contracted, and date confirmed.

facts.value: xAI/SpaceX Colossus — data center compute access
facts.excerpt: Paying up to $15 billion per year for xAI's Colossus data center access.
facts.notes: xAI/SpaceX-owned Colossus capacity rented by Anthropic — cloud_partnership. Figure ('up to $15 billion per year') is Epoch AI's characterization; no MW disclosed in this source. CoreWeave was also reported (Epoch/SemiAnalysis, April 2026) as an added compute partner but no fetched primary figure was captured.

verification.verifier_note: Epoch (May 20, 2026) states 'Anthropic has agreed to rent xAI's entire ~300,000 H100e Colossus 1 data center along with part of Colossus 2'. Compute-access/contracted status and date confirmed. Note: page attributes Colossus to xAI, not SpaceX.

facts.value: Fluidstack/TeraWulf — Abernathy, Texas (168 MW critical IT)
facts.excerpt: 168 MW critical IT load AI data center campus in Abernathy, Texas... TeraWulf... sold its entire 50.1% ownership interest... to Fluidstack.
facts.notes: Fluidstack/TeraWulf-owned (Anthropic is tenant/offtaker) — cloud_partnership, not Anthropic-owned. 168 MW critical IT (240 MW gross per secondary reporting), 120 acres, construction delivery targeted Q4 2026. Part of the Anthropic-Fluidstack $50B build-out. Publisher domain terawulf.com — flag for review.

verification.verifier_note: Same press release states 'Abernathy, Texas', '168 MW critical IT load', developed by 'TeraWulf and Fluidstack' JV (TeraWulf selling majority interest to Fluidstack). Described as under development. Location, 168 MW, Fluidstack/TeraWulf, and roughly under-construction status confirmed.

facts.value: TeraWulf — Justified Data Campus, Hawesville KY: 20-year lease, ~$19B contracted lease revenue, ~401 MW critical IT
facts.excerpt: The lease is expected to generate approximately $19 billion of contracted lease revenue over the initial term... The campus will accommodate approximately 401 MW of critical IT load
facts.notes: Adds dollar figure ($19B / 20-year term) to existing hawesville-ky entry which only had ~401 MW. Value = $19,000,000,000. Leased from TeraWulf (not owned) → cloud_partnership. Same release: TeraWulf sold its 50.1% Abernathy TX (168 MW) JV stake to a Fluidstack-led group for ~$530M.

verification.verifier_note: from gap-review pass; pending blind verification

facts.value: TeraWulf — Justified Data campus, Hawesville, Kentucky (~401 MW)
facts.excerpt: approximately 401 MW of critical IT load... 20-year lease agreement... approximately $19 billion of contracted lease revenue... ramping to the full 401 MW by early 2028.
facts.notes: TeraWulf-OWNED facility LEASED by Anthropic — attribute capacity to owner, so cloud_partnership not power_capacity_mw. 401 MW critical IT, initial capacity in service H2 2027, full 401 MW early 2028; $19B lease over 20-yr term. Publisher domain terawulf.com is the owner's newsroom (not in the pre-approved press list) — flag for review.

verification.verifier_note: TeraWulf press release (Jul 6, 2026) states Justified Data campus in Hawesville, Kentucky, 'approximately 401 MW of critical IT load', with Anthropic having 'executed a 20-year lease agreement'. Location, ~401 MW, contracted status, and date all confirmed.
